page: Amazon Web Services x1e.4xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance running on the Xen hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon E7-8880 v3 processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 16 dedicated vCPUs across 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 2.3 GHz and includes a 45 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 488.0 GB of system memory, yielding 30.5 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 480 GB NVMe SSD, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 2 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate a clear performance profile. Single-core CPU performance is positioned in the bottom 10% across stress-ng and Geekbench tests, reflecting the older architecture of the Intel Xeon E7-8880 v3. However, multi-core CPU performance scales to average levels, as shown by Passmark and Geekbench multi-core scores. Memory performance exhibits a stark contrast: small-block cached memory read bandwidth is average at 624.32 GB/sec, whereas large-block uncached read and write memory bandwidths fall into the bottom 10% at 14.66 GB/sec and 11.2 GB/sec respectively.

Given the massive memory-to-vCPU ratio, this instance offers high resource density for memory-bound workloads, though it trades off raw CPU compute speed and modern instruction sets like AVX-512. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for legacy enterprise applications requiring vast memory footprints without needing high-performance single-threaded execution. The hardware profile is best suited for memory-intensive databases, in-memory caching layers like Redis, and enterprise applications with large working sets.
description: Amazon Web Services x1e.4xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance designed for memory-intensive workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon E7-8880 v3 processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores, it features a massive 488.0 GB of RAM alongside a bundled 480 GB NVMe SSD. While single-core CPU benchmarks and uncached large-block memory bandwidth fall into the bottom 10%, multi-core performance and cached memory read speeds remain average. The instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. This hardware profile is highly specialized for workloads requiring substantial memory capacity relative to compute power, such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications with large memory footprints.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x1e.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 488.0 GB of RAM, and a 480 GB NVMe SSD, designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads and databases.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x1e.4xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 16 vCPUs, 488.0 GB of RAM, and a 480 GB NVMe SSD for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-intensive server featuring an exceptional memory-to-vCPU ratio and local NVMe SSD storage for large-scale caching and database workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E7-8880 v3 processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores
Massive 488.0 GB of system memory providing 30.5 GB of RAM per core
Bundled 480 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Average multi-core CPU and cached memory read performance
Best suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications
